question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ContractCallRequest with gasPrice=0 and a non-zero value field to bypass fee payment validation in ContractCallContext.validatePayerBalance(), causing the EVM to execute value-bearing transactions without any gas cost contribution, violating the fee design invariant?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user set gasPrice to Long.MAX_VALUE in ContractCallRequest (only constrained by @Min(0)) to cause arithmetic overflow in downstream fee calculation logic, resulting in a computed transaction fee that is outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: cleanup()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the cleanup() method's unconditional deletion of the tmp directory at startup to race-condition fee schedule file loading from SystemFileLoader, causing the node to fall back to genesis fee schedules and apply lower-than-intended transaction fees?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user send a ContractCallRequest with estimate=true and gas=15_000_000 to force BinaryGasEstimator.search() to run up to maxGasEstimateRetriesCount=20 iterations, each executing the contract at artificially high gas values, causing the returned gas estimate to be inflated beyond the actual execution cost and outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a ContractCallRequest with gas=21_000 (the @Min boundary) and estimate=true so that BinaryGasEstimator.search() returns hi=21_000 as the gas estimate regardless of actual execution cost, causing the fee to be computed at the minimum possible gas value outside of what the contract actually requires?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user set gasPrice=0 in ContractCallRequest so that ContractCallContext.validatePayerBalance() returns false (since gasPrice > 0 is required to trigger balance validation), allowing fee-free execution of contract calls that should incur a cost under design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the hardcoded CONTRACT_CREATE_TX_FEES=1_000_000_000L in TransactionExecutionService.buildContractCreateTransactionBody() by submitting a contract creation with a gas value far below what the fee should be, causing the fixed fee to be applied regardless of actual gas consumption and violating the fee-proportional design?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that buildContractCallTransactionBody() in TransactionExecutionService does NOT set a transactionFee field (unlike buildContractCreateTransactionBody()), and exploit this omission to submit contract calls where the effective transaction fee defaults to zero or an unintended value outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a historical block parameter in ContractCallRequest that maps to a timestamp before a fee schedule upgrade in SystemFileLoader.loadWithRetry(), causing the system to apply an older, lower fee schedule to the transaction and compute fees outside current design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the roundDownToHour() logic in SystemFileLoader to select a consensus timestamp that falls just after an hour boundary, causing the exchange rate cache to serve a stale rate from the previous hour and compute tinycent-to-tinybar conversions outside design parameters, resulting in incorrect fee amounts?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with a historical block timestamp that causes MidnightRatesSingleton.get() to load an exchange rate from a period with an extreme HBAR/USD ratio, causing the fee denominated in tinybars to be computed far outside current design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that CongestionLevelStartsSingleton.get() always returns CongestionLevelStarts.DEFAULT (no congestion) to bypass congestion-based fee multipliers, causing all transactions to be priced at base fee rates even during simulated high-load scenarios, violating the dynamic fee design?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the hardcoded fees.simpleFeesEnabled=false property in EvmProperties.buildTransactionProperties() to force the system to always use the complex fee schedule, and by crafting a call that hits a fee schedule entry with an unexpectedly low resource price, obtain execution at fees below design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user set estimate=true in ContractCallRequest to trigger the estimateGas() path in ContractExecutionService, which skips restoreGasToBucket() and metric recording during the binary search loop, effectively executing up to 20 contract calls without their gas being properly accounted for in fee calculations?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a contract call that causes safeCall() in BinaryGasEstimator to catch and suppress an exception, returning null and causing the binary search to use prevGasLimit instead of actual gasUsed, resulting in a gas estimate that is lower than the true execution cost and outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the scaleGas() integer division in ThrottleProperties (gas / 10_000) to submit requests with gas values just below the GAS_SCALE_FACTOR=10_000 threshold, causing scaleGas() to return 0 and the gas throttle bucket to consume zero tokens, bypassing gas-based fee throttling entirely?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the restoreGasToBucket() logic in ContractCallService, where a failed transaction restores gasLimitRefundPercent (default 100%) of gasLimit back to the bucket, by deliberately causing transaction failures to recover all consumed gas tokens and effectively execute transactions at zero net gas cost?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ContractCallRequest with value > 0 but from=null, exploiting the hasFrom() validation that only requires from to be non-null when value > 0, to trigger a default treasury account sender path in getSenderAccountID() that bypasses payer balance checks and executes value transfers without proper fee validation?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user set maxGasAllowance to Long.MAX_VALUE in buildEthereumTransactionBody() (hardcoded in TransactionExecutionService) by triggering the Ethereum transaction path via ContractDebugParameters, causing the fee cap to be effectively unlimited and allowing execution at fees outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the validatePayerBalance=false default in EvmProperties to submit transactions with any gasPrice value without the system verifying that the sender has sufficient balance to cover gas fees, allowing execution of transactions that would be rejected under proper fee enforcement?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a block parameter pointing to a historical timestamp where the fee schedule file (feeScheduleFile) had a parse error, causing SystemFileLoader.loadWithRetry() to fall back to the genesis fee schedule via retryTemplate, and execute the transaction under genesis-era fees that are lower than current design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the exchange rate file (exchangeRateFileId) is cached per hour via roundDownToHour() in SystemFileLoader, and by querying at a timestamp just before a rate update within the same hour, obtain a stale exchange rate that computes fees in tinybars at a rate outside current design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user set gas=15_000_000 (the default maximum) in ContractCallRequest and estimate=false to force the system to consume the maximum gas from the throttle bucket in a single request, then exploit the 100% gasLimitRefundPercent to restore all gas on failure, effectively making repeated maximum-gas calls at zero net throttle cost?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the binary search in BinaryGasEstimator.search() where the termination condition is lo + 1 < hi, causing the estimator to return hi (the upper bound) rather than the true minimum gas, inflating the gas estimate and causing the caller to overpay fees relative to actual execution cost, outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a contract that consistently returns gasUsed=0 in BinaryGasEstimator.search(), causing the condition gasUsed == 0 to set lo=mid on every iteration, driving the binary search to converge at hi (maximum gas) and return an inflated gas estimate that is outside design parameters?
